Document:
Space Law is a tool used to benefit the sector, not a cudgel. The greater good of the station is to be considered during sentencing. Space Law is designed to protect the station and crew, not to hinder them. Station-wide emergencies are to take priority over enforcing Space Law. Roleplay fun is to take priority over all others.
Read [textlink="RP-First Sentencing" link="https://docs.hardlight.space/space-law/security-overview.html"] in Security Overview before applying charges. Space Law is meant to support roleplay, reward cooperation, and avoid punitive maximization.
# Modifiers Modifiers adjust sentences up or down. Multiple modifiers may apply to a single charge.
[color=black][bold]Code[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Modifier[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Description[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Adjustment[/bold][/color]
[color=black]M01[/color]
[color=black][bold]Good Samaritan Clause[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Crimes committed in the interest of saving life, property, or preventing a disaster may have their sentences reduced, voided, or expunged.[/color]
[color=black]Voiding of charges[/color]
[color=black]M02[/color]
[color=black][bold]Elevated Sentencing[/bold][/color]
[color=black]The scope of the crime(s), or repetitive behavior committed, warrants the charge be elevated to a higher offence in increments of one level per instance.[/color]
[color=black]Decided by HoS, Warden, or Captain[/color]
[color=black]M03[/color]
[color=black][bold]Self Defense[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Actions taken to defend oneself from an assailant may have sentences reduced, voided, or expunged if the officer(s) involved deem the use of force reasonable.[/color]
[color=black]Voiding of charges[/color]
[color=black]M04[/color]
[color=black][bold]Non Compos Mentis[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Those of unstable mind should be referred to medical treatment or, if required, forced cryogenic storage.[/color]
[color=black]Forced medical treatment or cryogenic storage[/color]
[color=black]M05[/color]
[color=black][bold]Griseo Maris[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Coordinated behavior by those with no clear assigned duty which interrupts the normal function of any one department may lead to sentencing for crimes committed during such actions being increased by one level.[/color]
[color=black]Severity increased by one level[/color]
[color=black]M06[/color]
[color=black][bold]Resisting Arrest[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Knowingly resisting attempts at legal detention by members of the security team. Sentences should be increased by the recommended 20% of total duration.[/color]
[color=black]20% punishment increase[/color]
[color=black]M07[/color]
[color=black][bold]Escaped Prisoner[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Someone who has escaped their cell should be recaptured and returned to the brig. Their timer should be reset to the full length of the original sentence. If the prisoner is a Perma, they are to be stowed within Cryo.[/color]
[color=black]Reset of imprisonment time; if permanent confinement, cryogenic storage[/color]
[color=black]M08[/color]
[color=black][bold]Attempted Crime[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Anyone who tries to commit a Felony or below offense but fails to achieve it is to be charged under the relevant crime while recording that it was an attempt. Attempts warrant a 5% decrease in punishment.[/color]
[color=black]Same crime, 5% decrease in punishment[/color]
[color=black]M09[/color]
[color=black][bold]Hate Crime[/bold][/color]
[color=black]If the crime is committed to cause fear, injury, or harm due to racial, spiritual, physical, sexual orientation, gender identity, or other classifiable hate crime reasons. Cannot be applied to 111 — Discriminatory Language, as it is implied.[/color]
[color=black]50% time increase[/color]
[color=black]M10[/color]
[color=black][bold]Colonial Command Directive[/bold][/color]
[color=black]A pardon or sentence modification issued by Colonial Command. Colonial Command may waive sections of Space Law. If issued, it is to be followed immediately.[/color]
[color=black]Immediate release or modification[/color]
[color=black]M11[/color]
[color=black][bold]Medical Attention[/bold][/color]
[color=black]A sick or injured prisoner requires medical aid. Prisoners have the right to medical aid if injured or sick. In cases of self-harm, aid is discretionary by security. The timer continues running during medical aid.[/color]
[color=black]Running timer[/color]
[color=black]M12[/color]
[color=black][bold]Hostile Brainwashing[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To be under the mind control of a Cult, a head revolutionary, a vampire, or other mind-controlling circumstances. Brainwashed persons are to be detained non-lethally if possible and deconverted. Once deconverted, they are to be released.[/color]
[color=black]Deconversion and release[/color]
[color=black]M13[/color]
[color=black][bold]Cooperation with Security[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To completely comply and work with security during detainment, search, or arrest — such as willingly being cuffed or actively turning oneself in. In cases of Capital crimes, lowers punishment to Permanent Confinement.[/color]
[color=black]25% punishment decrease[/color]
## Sentencing Reference The following table defines the baseline sentencing range for each crime tier, to provide context when applying [bold]M02 — Elevated Sentencing[/bold] (which elevates a charge by one tier).
[color=black][bold]Tier[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Typical Sentence[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Infraction[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Fine only: 10–25k speso; no brig unless fine is evaded[/color]
[color=black][bold]Minor Crime[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Up to 10 min brig; fine optional[/color]
[color=black][bold]Misdemeanor[/bold][/color]
[color=black]5–15 min brig; 20–45k speso fine[/color]
[color=black][bold]Felony[/bold][/color]
[color=black]10–20 min brig; 35–85k speso fine; possible demotion[/color]
[color=black][bold]Capital Crime[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Permanent Confinement, Execution, or Force Borgification[/color]
## Lesser Included Offenses When two charges describe the same criminal act at different severities, only the greatest applicable charge should be applied. [bold]Stacking is not permitted.[/bold]
[color=black][bold]Crime Type[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Severity Chain (Greatest to Lesser Included)[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Homicide / Assault[/bold][/color]
[color=black]401 — Capital Murder > 301 — Murder > 201 — Aggravated Assault > 101 — Assault[/color]
[color=black][bold]Sabotage / Property Destruction[/bold][/color]
[color=black]403 — Grand Sabotage > 303 — Sabotage > 203 — Vandalism, Major[/color]
[color=black][bold]Breaking and Entering (Forcible Entry)[/bold][/color]
[color=black]310 — Breaking and Entering, High Security Areas > 210 — Breaking and Entering, Inherently Dangerous Areas > 110 — Breaking and Entering[/color]
[color=black][bold]Trespass (Unlawful Presence)[/bold][/color]
[color=black]309 — Trespass, High Security Areas > 209 — Trespass, Inherently Dangerous Areas > 109 — Trespass[/color]
[color=black][bold]Theft[/bold][/color]
[color=black]302 — Larceny > 202 — Theft > 102 — Pickpocketing[/color]
[color=black][bold]Sexual Offenses[/bold][/color]
[color=black]318 — Sexual Assault > 218 — Sexual Harassment[/color]
[bold]Stacking is not permitted.[/bold] Security may not charge both a greater offense and its lesser included offense for the same underlying act. The greatest offense should be charged, but may be reduced to a lesser included offense at officer discretion. [bold]Trespass and Breaking and Entering are legally distinct offenses.[/bold] 109/209/309 govern unlawful presence in a restricted area; 110/210/310 govern the act of forcible entry. When both the unlawful entry and the unlawful presence are established, both charges may apply. ---
# Infractions All of these crimes tend to be monetary punishments.
[color=black][bold]Code[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Crime[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Description[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Punishment[/bold][/color]
[color=black]003[/color]
[color=black][bold]Superficial Vandalism[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Defaced station property or property belonging to another individual[/color]
[color=black]Force repairing or 15k speso fine[/color]
[color=black]006[/color]
[color=black][bold]Drug Possession[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Possessed illegal drugs; In quantities consistent with personal use[/color]
[color=black]Confiscation and 25k speso fine[/color]
[color=black]007[/color]
[color=black][bold]Disorderly Conduct[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Conducted oneself in a manner unsuitable for their position[/color]
[color=black]Brig until 25k speso fine paid; with M02: demotion[/color]
[color=black]008[/color]
[color=black][bold]Creating a Hazard[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Created a condition capable of causing harm to others; During normal departmental operations[/color]
[color=black]Forced removal of situation and 15k speso fine[/color]
[color=black]011[/color]
[color=black][bold]Disruptive Comms[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Knowingly disrupted or needlessly populated a radio frequency; With intent to be a nuisance[/color]
[color=black]Brig until fine paid (unless M02); 10k speso fine[/color]
# Minor Crimes These crimes tend to be just brig time.
[color=black][bold]Code[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Crime[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Description[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Punishment[/bold][/color]
[color=black]101[/color]
[color=black][bold]Assault[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Used physical force against another person; Without apparent intent to kill[/color]
[color=black]Restraining and separation; 5 min brig[/color]
[color=black]102[/color]
[color=black][bold]Pickpocketing[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Stole an item; Directly from another person's person (pocket, hand, or worn equipment)[/color]
[color=black]Return of item(s) and 5 min brig[/color]
[color=black]103[/color]
[color=black][bold]Minor Vandalism[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Damaged, destroyed, or permanently defaced; Non-critical furniture, a vendor, or personal property[/color]
[color=black]Work duty to repair damage; brig time varies by damage[/color]
[color=black]104[/color]
[color=black][bold]Vigilantism[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Performed the responsibilities or duties of the security department; Without approval or due cause[/color]
[color=black]5 min brig[/color]
[color=black]106[/color]
[color=black][bold]Illegal Distribution[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Possessed illegal or dangerous drugs or equipment; In quantities beyond reasonable personal use[/color]
[color=black]5 min brig and 40k fine[/color]
[color=black]107[/color]
[color=black][bold]Disturbing the Peace[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Engaged in conduct that a reasonable person would find clearly disruptive, threatening, or harmful to public order; Including: excessive noise, public fighting, or abusive language that is directed, repeated, and likely to provoke immediate confrontation (mere insults alone are insufficient); Or organized activity that materially disrupts normal operations[/color]
[color=black]Warning first; then 5–10 min brig or 15k speso fine[/color]
[color=black]108[/color]
[color=black][bold]Criminal Negligence[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Failed in their duty to a negligent degree; Caused or risked harm, illness, or negative effects to another; Did not result in death[/color]
[color=black]5 min brig[/color]
[color=black]109[/color]
[color=black][bold]Trespass[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Present in an area without admission or access; Or remained after being asked to leave by department staff[/color]
[color=black]Peacefully leaving: release. Otherwise: 5 min brig, or time equal to time spent trespassing if greater than 5 min[/color]
[color=black]110[/color]
[color=black][bold]Breaking and Entering[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Used a method of forcible entry to trespass into an area; Includes non-destructive methods (hacking, bypassing access controls); Distinct from simple trespass and charged as the primary entry offense when forcible entry is proven[/color]
[color=black]Baseline 7.5 min brig (50% above 109), increased further by damage caused[/color]
[color=black]111[/color]
[color=black][bold]Discriminatory Language[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Used language targeting an individual; That demeans, generalizes, or de-personifies them[/color]
[color=black]10 min brig[/color]
[color=black]112[/color]
[color=black][bold]Fine Evasion[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Purposefully avoided, refused, or was unable to pay a legal fine; After reasonable notice and opportunity to pay[/color]
[color=black]15 sec per 1k speso owed (e.g., 30k fine = 7.5 min brig)[/color]
[color=black]116[/color]
[color=black][bold]Brandishing[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Brandished a weapon; Without lawful purpose or due cause[/color]
[color=black]5 min brig or a person search[/color]
[color=black]117[/color]
[color=black][bold]Harassment[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Repeatedly targeted, followed, contacted, provoked, or otherwise engaged with another individual; After being clearly told to stop; Creating a hostile, intimidating, or disruptive environment[/color]
[color=black]5–10 min brig, 15k speso fine[/color]
[color=black]118[/color]
[color=black][bold]Failure to Identify[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Refused to provide name, job, or identification; During lawful questioning or detainment by security; Or failed to wear a valid ID card while on Blue Alert or higher[/color]
[color=black]Warning; 5 min brig if refusal continues[/color]
[color=black]119[/color]
[color=black][bold]Resist, Delay, or Obstruct Justice[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Resisted, delayed, or obstructed a lawful arrest, investigation, evidence collection, prisoner transport, or security operation; Including: physically impeding officers, providing false information, "slipping" officers, failing to comply with reasonable requests to vacate or create space near officers or a crime scene, or any conduct that distracts or hinders Security response[/color]
[color=black]5–10 min brig, fine optional[/color]
# Misdemeanors These crimes tend to include both fines and brig time.
[color=black][bold]Code[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Crime[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Description[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Punishment[/bold][/color]
[color=black]201[/color]
[color=black][bold]Aggravated Assault[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Took physical action against a person; With intent to grievously harm; But without intent to kill[/color]
[color=black]10 min brig, flat 25k fine[/color]
[color=black]202[/color]
[color=black][bold]Theft[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Stole equipment or items from a workplace; Or items of extraordinary value from a person's person[/color]
[color=black]Return of items, 5 min brig, 20k fine[/color]
[color=black]203[/color]
[color=black][bold]Vandalism, Major[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Destroyed or damaged non-critical furniture, vendors, or personal property; In a manner that cannot be repaired[/color]
[color=black]Replacement of item(s), monetary recompensation, 5 min brig[/color]
[color=black]204[/color]
[color=black][bold]Conspiracy[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Knowingly planned or conspired to commit a misdemeanor or greater offense; With intent to carry that offense out[/color]
[color=black]5–10 min brig (discretionary)[/color]
[color=black]205[/color]
[color=black][bold]Hostile Agent[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Knowingly supported, aided, or associated with a hostile organization; In a non-combat capacity: recruitment, messaging, smuggling, harboring, or financing; (see 405 — Enemy of the Government)[/color]
[color=black]5 min brig, 25k fine[/color]
[color=black]206[/color]
[color=black][bold]Possession, Contraband Equipment[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Possessed Class 2 contraband equipment; Without a valid C2 Permit[/color]
[color=black]5 min brig, 25k fine, confiscation[/color]
[color=black]207[/color]
[color=black][bold]Rioting[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Acted as a member of a group; That collectively committed major vandalism, sabotage, or other felony crimes[/color]
[color=black]HoS or Warden's judgement[/color]
[color=black]208[/color]
[color=black][bold]Gross Criminal Negligence[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Negligent in their duty; Caused harm to multiple individuals or a department; Or directly led to serious injury requiring emergency medical treatment[/color]
[color=black]Baseline 10 min brig (varies by severity), possible demotion, 30k speso fine[/color]
[color=black]209[/color]
[color=black][bold]Trespass, Inherently Dangerous Areas[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Trespassed in an area that may lead to injury of oneself or others; Includes No-Fly Zones (NFZs)[/color]
[color=black]15 min brig, 25k speso fine[/color]
[color=black]210[/color]
[color=black][bold]Breaking and Entering, Inherently Dangerous Areas[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Used forcible entry to trespass; Into an area that may lead to injury of oneself or others[/color]
[color=black]10 min brig (potentially increased by damage), 30k speso fine[/color]
[color=black]211[/color]
[color=black][bold]Insubordination[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Knowingly disobeyed a lawful order; From a superior or security officer; Without good reason[/color]
[color=black]5 min brig, possible demotion, 25k fine[/color]
[color=black]212[/color]
[color=black][bold]Fraud[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Misrepresented their intentions; With the purpose of gaining property or money from another individual[/color]
[color=black]10 min brig, return of property or money, 45k fine[/color]
[color=black]213[/color]
[color=black][bold]Mutilation[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Purposefully modified a person without consent, or with intent to harm; Includes minor medical malpractice[/color]
[color=black]Reversal of operations if applicable, 5 min brig[/color]
[color=black]214[/color]
[color=black][bold]Evidence Tampering[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Destroyed, concealed, altered, planted, or stole evidence; Related to an active or reasonably anticipated investigation[/color]
[color=black]10 min brig, 30k fine[/color]
[color=black]215[/color]
[color=black][bold]False Imprisonment[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Unlawfully arrested, detained, confined, or held a person; Includes holding an individual beyond their lawful sentence; Without legal authority to do so[/color]
[color=black]5 min brig, 20k fine[/color]
[color=black]216[/color]
[color=black][bold]False Report[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Knowingly submitted a false criminal report, false witness statement, or false emergency call[/color]
[color=black]5–10 min brig, 25k fine[/color]
[color=black]217[/color]
[color=black][bold]Intimidation[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Made a credible threat of violent action against a specific person; That a reasonable person in the victim’s position would believe could realistically be carried out; Victim must agree to press charges[/color]
[color=black]5 min brig, 15k fine (increased to 35k if means to follow up are found on their person)[/color]
[color=black]218[/color]
[color=black][bold]Sexual Harassment[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Engaged in unwelcome conduct of a sexual nature (physical touching, verbal comments, or non-verbal gestures); That either: violated the victim’s dignity, OR created a hostile, intimidating, or offensive environment[/color]
[color=black]5 min brig, 10k fine[/color]
[color=black]219[/color]
[color=black][bold]Unlawful Search[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Conducted a search of a person, vessel, or department; Without lawful cause, consent, a valid warrant, or emergency justification[/color]
[color=black]5 min brig, possible demotion[/color]
[color=black]220[/color]
[color=black][bold]Abuse of Authority[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Used a position of command, security, medical, or legal authority; To harass, extort, falsely arrest, or unlawfully punish another individual[/color]
[color=black]10 min brig, demotion[/color]
[color=black]221[/color]
[color=black][bold]Unlawful Butchering of Fauna[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Killed or caused the death of a non-sentient creature aboard the station; That posed no threat to the individual or others at the time; Including station pets, common friendly station fauna, and similar non-hostile creatures[/color]
[color=black]5–10 min brig, 20k speso fine; restitution where applicable[/color]
# Felonies These crimes tend to have both heavy fines and brig time as punishments.
[color=black][bold]Code[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Crime[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Description[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Punishment[/bold][/color]
[color=black]301[/color]
[color=black][bold]Murder[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Intentionally killed another person with malice[/color]
[color=black]10–20 min brig, 40–60k speso fine[/color]
[color=black]302[/color]
[color=black][bold]Larceny[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Stole rare, expensive (>50k speso value), or restricted equipment; From a secure area or directly from a person[/color]
[color=black]10 min brig, return of items, 40k speso fine[/color]
[color=black]303[/color]
[color=black][bold]Sabotage[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Destroyed station assets or resources critical to normal or emergency station procedures; Or caused sections of the station to become uninhabitable[/color]
[color=black]10 min brig, 40k speso fine[/color]
[color=black]304[/color]
[color=black][bold]Grand Conspiracy[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Knowingly planned or conspired to commit a Felony or Capital crime; With intent to carry that offense out[/color]
[color=black]Same punishment as the associated crime[/color]
[color=black]305[/color]
[color=black][bold]Hostile Activity[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Committed a direct hostile act against the station, crew, or Colonial Command; In coordination with or on behalf of a hostile organization[/color]
[color=black]10 min brig, 20k speso fine[/color]
[color=black]306[/color]
[color=black][bold]Possession, Illegal Inherently Dangerous Equipment[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Possessed restricted or illegal equipment; With a primary purpose of causing harm or large-scale destruction; C3 equipment is included[/color]
[color=black]Item removal, 10 min brig, 35k speso fine, mandatory shuttle search[/color]
[color=black]307[/color]
[color=black][bold]Inciting a Riot[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Performed actions intended to cause large-scale unrest; Up to and including facilitating or causing rioting[/color]
[color=black]10 min brig, 45–60k speso fine[/color]
[color=black]308[/color]
[color=black][bold]Manslaughter[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Unintentionally killed another person; Through negligent but non-malicious actions[/color]
[color=black]10 min brig, 35–50k speso fine[/color]
[color=black]309[/color]
[color=black][bold]Trespass, High Security Areas[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Trespassed without permission; In Command areas, personal offices, weapons/explosive storage or production, or other high security areas; Includes NT Vaults, Syndicate caches, and NFZ-covered space[/color]
[color=black]15 min brig, return of items taken, 45–60k speso fine; NFZ violation adds half the time spent in NFZ[/color]
[color=black]310[/color]
[color=black][bold]Breaking and Entering, High Security Areas[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Used forcible entry to trespass; Into a high security area as defined under 309 — Trespass, High Security Areas[/color]
[color=black]As per 210, plus an additional 10k speso fine[/color]
[color=black]311[/color]
[color=black][bold]Dereliction[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Willfully abandoned an obligation; Critical to the station's continued operation[/color]
[color=black]Demotion, 40k speso fine[/color]
[color=black]312[/color]
[color=black][bold]Embezzlement[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Embezzled funds from station fund computers[/color]
[color=black]Return of funds, 40k fine, demotion, 15 min brig[/color]
[color=black]313[/color]
[color=black][bold]Illegal Mindbreaking[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Permanently removed a psion's abilities; Not as a lawful punishment, by a Mantis in the line of duty, or during an extenuating emergency[/color]
[color=black]15 min brig, 85k fine, restoration of psion's powers if able, removal of perpetrator's powers if applicable[/color]
[color=black]314[/color]
[color=black][bold]Impersonation[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Assumed the identity of another individual or job[/color]
[color=black]Removal of items used to impersonate, 40k speso fine, 10 min brig[/color]
[color=black]315[/color]
[color=black][bold]Kidnapping[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Unlawfully abducted a person; Transported them to a new location; Confined them against their will[/color]
[color=black]10 min brig, 70k fine[/color]
[color=black]317[/color]
[color=black][bold]Coercion[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Offered or accepted a bribe as or to a contracted station member; Or blackmailed someone[/color]
[color=black]10 min brig, removal of bribe money or items, 40k fine[/color]
[color=black]318[/color]
[color=black][bold]Sexual Assault[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Engaged in an involuntary, non-consensual sexual act; Ranging from unwanted touching to forced penetration; Violating bodily autonomy[/color]
[color=black]15 min brig, 40k fine[/color]
[color=black]319[/color]
[color=black][bold]Unauthorized Implantation / Deimplantation[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Implanted, removed, tracked, mindshielded, or otherwise physically modified another individual; Without their informed consent or lawful authority[/color]
[color=black]10–15 min brig, 40k fine, reversal if possible[/color]
[color=black]320[/color]
[color=black][bold]Gross Medical Malpractice[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Committed severe medical misconduct; Resulting in death, permanent disability, wrongful Borgification, organ loss, or major irreversible harm; Through willful disregard or gross negligence[/color]
[color=black]10–15 min brig, demotion, 50k fine[/color]
# Capital Crimes These crimes tend to have extreme punishments.
[bold]Code[/bold]
[bold]Crime[/bold]
[bold]Description[/bold]
[bold]Punishment[/bold]
401
[bold]Capital Murder[/bold]
Committed murder with clear intent to kill; And materially took steps to prevent the victim's revival; Or forced Borging or discorporealization without consent or authorization
Execution, Permanent Confinement, or Force Borgification
403
[bold]Grand Sabotage[/bold]
Destroyed or modified station assets or equipment; Without which the station may collapse or become entirely uninhabitable
Permanent Confinement, Demotion
404
[bold]Espionage[/bold]
Knowingly betrayed critical station information; To enemies of the station
Permanent Confinement
405
[bold]Enemy of the Government[/bold]
Is or was a confirmed member of a hostile organization: True Nexus, Xenoborg Collective, hostile boarding parties, Changeling Hiveminds, cults, or Dirizher Cartel
Permanent Confinement or transportation to Colonial Command for interrogation
407
[bold]Subversion of the Chain of Command[/bold]
Disrupted the chain of command via the murder of a commanding officer; Or illegally declared oneself to be a commanding officer
Permanent Confinement; Execution if Criminal is a Head Revolutionary
408
[bold]Biological Terror[/bold]
Knowingly released or caused the station to be affected by a disease, plant, or biological form; That may spread uncontained and/or cause serious physical harm
Permanent Confinement, Execution, or Force Borgification
411
[bold]Grand Dereliction[/bold]
As security or command, acted in a way detrimental to the station, sector, or Colonial Command; Examples: selling station gear to spacers, intentionally permitting or encouraging felony or capital crimes
Permanent Confinement, transportation to Colonial Command, Execution in extreme cases
415
[bold]Grand Kidnapping[/bold]
Unlawfully abducted a Command member or higher authority; And confined them against their will
Permanent Confinement
416
[bold]Perfidy[/bold]
Intentionally baited Security or Command with a peaceful resolution; Then betrayed their trust to elude capture or launch a surprise attack
Permanent Confinement
417
[bold]Brainwashing[/bold]
Brainwashed someone non-consensually; Or caused them to commit crimes under mind control
Punishments applied for crimes committed by the brainwashed; Permanent Confinement, Forced Borgification, or Execution
